mission.title: Install and Configure ExternalDNS
mission.description: ExternalDNS is a Kubernetes operator that dynamically manages DNS records for Kubernetes resources such as Services and Ingresses. It is particularly useful for environments where services are frequently created and destroyed, allowing for automatic DNS updates without manual intervention. Key trade-offs include the need for proper permissions and potential delays in DNS propagation.

steps.title: Add Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the ExternalDNS Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add external-dns https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami

```

> This is the official Bitnami chart repository for ExternalDNS.

steps.title: Install ExternalDNS
steps.description:
This command installs ExternalDNS in the 'kube-system' namespace with the specified version.

```bash

helm install external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --version 0.20.0 --set provider=aws --set aws.zoneType=public

```

> Replace 'aws' with your DNS provider as needed.
commands: helm install external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --version 0.20.0 --set provider=aws --set aws.zoneType=public

steps.title: Create ExternalDNS Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Custom Resource (CR) for ExternalDNS to manage DNS records based on your Kubernetes resources.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: externaldns.k8s.io/v1alpha1
kind: ExternalDNS
metadata:
  name: external-dns
spec:
  domainFilters:
    - example.com
  provider: aws
  policy: upsert-only
  service: true
  ingress: true
EOF

```

> Replace 'example.com' with your actual domain.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: externaldns.k8s.io/v1alpha1
kind: ExternalDNS
metadata:
  name: external-dns
spec:
  domainFilters:
    - example.com
  provider: aws
  policy: upsert-only
  service: true
  ingress: true
EOF

steps.title: Verify ExternalDNS Installation
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the ExternalDNS deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-dns

```

> You should see the ExternalDNS pod in a Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall ExternalDNS
uninstall.description:
This command removes ExternalDNS from your cluster. Be aware that this will also remove any DNS records managed by ExternalDNS, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall external-dns --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete customresourcedefinition externaldns.k8s.io

```

upgrade.title: Upgrade ExternalDNS
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your current configuration. This step will upgrade ExternalDNS to the latest version.

```bash

helm upgrade external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --version 0.20.0

kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-dns

```

> Ensure to check for any breaking changes in the release notes.
> Verify that the new version is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create record set'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient permissions for the DNS provider.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the IAM role or service account used by ExternalDNS has the necessary permissions to create DNS records.

troubleshooting.title: ExternalDNS pod is CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if there are misconfigurations in the ExternalDNS deployment.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the ExternalDNS pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-system' for detailed error messages.

troubleshooting.title: DNS records not updating
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may be due to incorrect domain filters or misconfigured provider settings.

**Fix:**
Verify the ExternalDNS configuration and ensure that the domain filters match your DNS zones.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No valid zones found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that ExternalDNS cannot find the specified DNS zones in the provider.

**Fix:**
Check your provider settings and ensure that the zones are correctly configured and accessible.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new DNS providers and improved handling of multiple zones.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the '--zone-type' flag in favor of 'aws.zoneType'.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.15, ensure to update your configuration to use the new flags.

resolution.summary: You should see the ExternalDNS pod running with the command 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=external-dns' returning a status of 'Running'.
codeSnippets: helm install external-dns external-dns/external-dns --namespace kube-system --version 0.20.0 --set provider=aws --set aws.zoneType=public

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF

kind: ExternalDNS
metadata:
  name: external-dns
spec:

    - example.com
  provider: aws
  policy: upsert-only
  service: true
  ingress: true
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
